Market SizeTAM, SAM, SOM Diagram with Total Available, Serviceable Available & Obtainable Market Volume [![Image 1: infoDiagram.com](https://www.infodiagram.com/_ipx/s_271x61/images/logo.svg)![Image 2: infoDiagram.com](https://www.infodiagram.com/_ipx/s_36x36/images/infoDiagram-logo-sygnet.svg)](https://www.infodiagram.com/) *   [![Image 3: Chat](https://www.infodiagram.com/images/icons/icon-ai-advisor.png) AI Slide Advisor](https://www.infodiagram.com/chat) Presentation Templates      *   [New and Updated](https://www.infodiagram.com/catalog/new)     *   [Bundles](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/bundles)     *   Popular categories     *   [Climate, Sustainability, ESG](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/ecology-ppt-templates)     *   [Data Analytics, IT](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/it-data-analytics-ppt-templates)     *   [Finance](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/finance-ppt-templates)     *   [Human Resources](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/hr-ppt-templates)     *   [Management](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/management-ppt-templates)     *   [Medical & HealthCare](https://www.infodiagram.com/category/medical-health-care-ppt-templates)     *   [Free Slide Sample to Try](https://www.infodiagram.com", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Serviceable Available Market (SAM): How to Calculate It in 2026", "url": "https://prospeo.io/s/serviceable-available-market", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: Serviceable Available Market (SAM): How to Calculate It in 2026 URL Source: https://prospeo.io/s/serviceable-available-market Markdown Content: ## How to Calculate Your Serviceable Available Market (SAM) - With Real Examples You've got no customers, no revenue, and an investor just asked for your SAM. The math itself is simple. It's finding accurate inputs that kills most founders. Here's how to build a defensible [serviceable available market](https://www.salesforce.com/blog/tam-sam-som/) number from scratch, with real worked examples and the data sources that actually work. ## What You Need (Quick Version) **SAM in one sentence:** the slice of your total market you can realistically sell to today, given your product, geography, and go-to-market constraints. **The formula (most common bottom-up version):** Target accounts/customers x [annual value per account (ACV)](https://stripe.com/resources/more/annual-contract-value-acv-in-saas) = SAM. If you price per seat or usage, expand it: target accounts x average seats/usage x price. **The key insight:** bottom-up beats top-down every time. Saying \"we'll capture 1% of a $1B market\" is hand-waving. Counting the actual companies you can serve and multiplying by what you charge is defensible. **Where to get company counts:**[Census Bureau data](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/cbp.html) gives you broad industry totals. B2B databases let you filter by [ICP](https://prospeo.io/s/ideal-customer-profile-template) criteria - industry, headcount, geography, tech stack - to get the specific segment count you need for bottom-up math. Jump to: [Worked Examples](https://prospeo.io/s/serviceable-available-market#worked-sam-examples) | [Data Sources](https://prospeo.io/s/serviceable-available-market#where-to-get-sam-data) | [No Sales Data?](https://prospeo.io/s/serviceable-available-market#sam-with-zero-sales-data) ## What Is Serviceable Available Market? Your serviceable available market is the portion of the [total addressable market](https://prospeo.io/s/addressable-market) (TAM) that your business can actually reach and serve with its current product, pricing, and distribution. It's the revenue opportunity inside the segment you can serve today - not the segment you hope to serve in three years after a Series B and two pivots. ![Image 1: TAM SAM SOM concentric circles diagram with definitions](https://static.prospeo.io/directory-assets/images/new_images/serviceable-available-market/tam-sam-som-concentric-circles-diagram.png) TAM SAM SOM concentric circles diagram with definitions \"Serviceable Available Market\" and \"Serviceable Addressable Market\" are used interchangeably across the industry. \"Available\" emphasizes what's reachable now; \"Addressable\" emphasizes what you can target. Same metric, different emphasis. Don't let anyone tell you they're different concepts.\n...\nTitle: Serviceable Available Market (SAM): How to Calculate It in 2026 URL Source: https://prospeo.io/s/serviceable-available-mar", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Total Addressable Market - What Is TAM & How to Calculate It ...", "url": "https://www.toptal.com/management-consultants/market-sizing/total-addressable-market-example", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: TAM Methodology: An Explanation and Example of Total Addressable Market Analysis URL Source: https://www.toptal.com/management-consultants/market-sizing/total-addressable-market-example Published Time: 2017-08-31T12:28:00-0400 Markdown Content: ## Key Highlights What is TAM? *   Total addressable market (TAM) demonstrates the entire revenue opportunity that exists within a market for a product or service. *   There are four methods for calculating TAM: top-down approach, bottom-up approach, value theory, and referring to external research. *   The top-down approach consists of using macroeconomic factors to define a subset of customers that are unified by characteristics of using a product or service. Data providers such as the OECD, UN, World Bank, and CIA World Factbook are useful resources for determining this. *   Bottom-up analysis revolves around determining a local market size and then extrapolating it upwards to a wider population. It is perceived as more accurate as the data collected is primary, but its influence wanes over disparate geographies and cultures. *   Value theory is useful for completely new concepts of products/services that will gain users from being a positive trade-off versus incumbent options. It estimates the value-add (tangible and intangible) provided by the new offering and uses that to assume a theoretical revenue opportunity. *   Research companies, such as Gartner or IDC, publish reports that often postulate upon sectoral TAMs. This data has been professionally compiled, is credible and is quick to find; however, using it can compromise your ability to understand your market deeper via proprietary research. What is SAM? *   In the absence of monopoly control and/or unlimited financial resources, capturing 100% of a TAM is almost impossible for a business. *   Serviceable available market (SAM) carves the TAM up into sub-markets that cater towards different types of users. This can be defined by (but not limited to) differences in quality, price or functionality. *   Aside from competitive and financial limitations, other factors that lower a TAM into a SAM are geographic, cultural, regulatory, and product cannibalization considerations.\n...\nTitle: TAM Methodology: An Explanation and Example of Total Addressable Market Analysis URL Source: https://www.toptal.com/management-consultants/market-sizing/total-addressable-market-example Published Time: 2017-08-31T12:28:00-0400 Markdown Content: ## Key Highlights What is TAM? *   Total addressable market (TAM) demonstrates the entire revenue opportunity that exists within a market for a product or service. *   There are four methods for calculating TAM: top-down approach, bottom-up approach, value theory, and referring to external research. *   The top-down approach consists of using macroeconomic factors to define a subset of customers that are unified by characteristics of using a product or service. Data providers such as the OECD, UN, World Bank, and CIA Worl", "relevance_score": 0.85}]
